en: Pursue love, and earnestly desire the spiritual gifts, especially that you may prophesy.
zh: 你們要追求愛，也要熱切地渴慕屬靈的恩賜，特別是先知講道的恩賜。

en: For one who speaks in a tongue speaks not to men but to God; for no one understands him, but he utters mysteries in the Spirit.
zh: 原來那說方言的不是對人說，而是對 神說，因為沒有人能聽得懂；他是在靈裡講奧祕的事。

en: On the other hand, the one who prophesies speaks to people for their upbuilding and encouragement and consolation.
zh: 但那講道的是對人講說，使他們得著造就、安慰和勸勉。

en: The one who speaks in a tongue builds up himself, but the one who prophesies builds up the church.
zh: 那說方言的是造就自己，但那講道的是造就教會。

en: Now I want you all to speak in tongues, but even more to prophesy. The one who prophesies is greater than the one who speaks in tongues, unless someone interprets, so that the church may be built up.
zh: 我願意你們都說方言，但我更願意你們都講道；因為那說方言的，如果不翻譯出來使教會得著造就，就遠不如那講道的了。

en: Now, brothers, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I benefit you unless I bring you some revelation or knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
zh: 弟兄們，你們想想，如果我到你們那裡去，只說方言，不向你們講有關啟示、知識、預言，或教訓的話，那我對你們有甚麼益處呢？

en: If even lifeless instruments, such as the flute or the harp, do not give distinct notes, how will anyone know what is played?
zh: 甚至那些沒有生命卻能發聲的東西，例如簫或琴，如果音調不分，怎能使人知道所彈所奏的是甚麼呢？

en: And if the bugle gives an indistinct sound, who will get ready for battle?
zh: 又如果軍號所發的聲音不清楚，誰會準備作戰呢？

en: So with yourselves, if with your tongue you utter speech that is not intelligible, how will anyone know what is said? For you will be speaking into the air.
zh: 你們也是這樣，如果用舌頭發出人聽不懂的話來，人怎會知道你所講的是甚麼呢？這樣，你們就是向空氣說話了。

en: There are doubtless many different languages in the world, and none is without meaning,
zh: 世上有那麼多種語言，但沒有一種是沒有意義的。

en: but if I do not know the meaning of the language, I will be a foreigner to the speaker and the speaker a foreigner to me.
zh: 我若不明白某一種語言的意思，在那講的人來看，我就是個外國人；在我來說，那講話的人也是個外國人。

en: So with yourselves, since you are eager for manifestations of the Spirit, strive to excel in building up the church.
zh: 你們也是這樣，你們既然熱切地渴慕屬靈的恩賜，就應當追求多多得著造就教會的恩賜。

en: Therefore, one who speaks in a tongue should pray for the power to interpret.
zh: 所以，說方言的應當祈求，使他能把方言翻譯出來。

en: For if I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind is unfruitful.
zh: 我若用方言禱告，是我的靈在禱告，我的理智並沒有作用。

en: What am I to do? I will pray with my spirit, but I will pray with my mind also; I will sing praise with my spirit, but I will sing with my mind also.
zh: 那麼我應當怎樣行呢？我要用靈禱告，也要用理智禱告；我要用靈歌唱，也要用理智歌唱。

en: Otherwise, if you give thanks with your spirit, how can anyone in the position of an outsider say "Amen" to your thanksgiving when he does not know what you are saying?
zh: 不然，如果你用靈讚美，在場那些不明白的人，因為不知道你在說甚麼，怎能在你感謝的時候說“阿們”呢？

en: For you may be giving thanks well enough, but the other person is not being built up.
zh: 你感謝固然是好，但別人卻得不著造就。

en: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
zh: 我感謝 神，我說方言比你們大家都多。

en: Nevertheless, in church I would rather speak five words with my mind in order to instruct others, than ten thousand words in a tongue.
zh: 但在教會中，我寧願用理智說五句話去教導人，勝過用方言說萬句話。

en: Brothers, do not be children in your thinking. Be infants in evil, but in your thinking be mature.
zh: 弟兄們，你們在思想上不要作小孩子，卻要在惡事上作嬰孩，在思想上作成年人。

en: In the Law it is written, "By people of strange tongues and by the lips of foreigners will I speak to this people, and even then they will not listen to me, says the Lord."
zh: 律法上記著說：“主說： 我要藉著說別種話的人， 用外國人的嘴唇， 對這人民說話； 雖然這樣，他們還是不聽我。”

en: Thus tongues are a sign not for believers but for unbelievers, while prophecy is a sign not for unbelievers but for believers.
zh: 可見說方言不是要給信主的人作記號，而是要給未信的人；講道不是要給未信的人，而是要給信主的人作記號。

en: If, therefore, the whole church comes together and all speak in tongues, and outsiders or unbelievers enter, will they not say that you are out of your minds?
zh: 所以，如果全教會聚在一起的時候，大家都說方言，有不明白的人或未信的人進來，不是要說你們瘋了嗎？

en: But if all prophesy, and an unbeliever or outsider enters, he is convicted by all, he is called to account by all,
zh: 如果大家都講道，有未信的人或不明白的人進來，他就會被眾人勸服而知罪，被眾人審問了。

en: the secrets of his heart are disclosed, and so, falling on his face, he will worship God and declare that God is really among you.
zh: 他心裡隱祕的事被顯露出來，他就必俯伏敬拜 神，宣告說：“ 神真的是在你們中間了。”

en: What then, brothers? When you come together, each one has a hymn, a lesson, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. Let all things be done for building up.
zh: 弟兄們，那麼應該怎麼辦呢？你們聚集在一起的時候，各人或有詩歌，或有教訓，或有啟示，或有方言，或有翻譯出來的話，一切都應該能造就人。

en: If any speak in a tongue, let there be only two or at most three, and each in turn, and let someone interpret.
zh: 如果有人說方言，只可以有兩個人，或最多三個人，並且要輪流說，同時要有一個人翻譯。

en: But if there is no one to interpret, let each of them keep silent in church and speak to himself and to God.
zh: 如果沒有人翻譯，他就應當在會中閉口，只對自己和對 神說好了。

en: Let two or three prophets speak, and let the others weigh what is said.
zh: 講道的，也只可以兩三個人講，其餘的人要衡量他們所講的。

en: If a revelation is made to another sitting there, let the first be silent.
zh: 在座的有人得了啟示，那先講的人就應當住口。

en: For you can all prophesy one by one, so that all may learn and all be encouraged,
zh: 因為你們都可以輪流講道，好讓大家都可以學習，都可以得到勉勵。

en: and the spirits of prophets are subject to prophets.
zh: 先知的靈是受先知控制的，

en: For God is not a God of confusion but of peace. As in all the churches of the saints,
zh: 因為 神不是混亂的，而是和平的。

en: the women should keep silent in the churches. For they are not permitted to speak, but should be in submission, as the Law also says.
zh: 婦女在聚會中應當閉口，好像在聖徒的眾教會中一樣，因為她們是不准講話的；就如律法所說的，她們應該順服。

en: If there is anything they desire to learn, let them ask their husbands at home. For it is shameful for a woman to speak in church.
zh: 如果她們想要學甚麼，可以在家裡問自己的丈夫，因為婦女在聚會中講話原是可恥的。

en: Or was it from you that the word of God came? Or are you the only ones it has reached?
zh: 難道 神的道是從你們出來的嗎？是單單臨到你們的嗎？

en: If anyone thinks that he is a prophet, or spiritual, he should acknowledge that the things I am writing to you are a command of the Lord.
zh: 如果有人自以為是先知或是屬靈的，他就應該知道我寫給你們的是主的命令；

en: If anyone does not recognize this, he is not recognized.
zh: 如果有人不理會，別人也不必理會他。

en: So, my brothers, earnestly desire to prophesy, and do not forbid speaking in tongues.
zh: 所以我的弟兄們，你們要熱切地追求講道的恩賜，也不要禁止說方言。

en: But all things should be done decently and in order.
zh: 凡事都要規規矩矩地按著次序行。
